summary: Comprehensive backend + frontend test of PharmaSignal DSP. 14/14 pytest backend tests pass on first invocation. Frontend dashboard, audiences, PMPs, data-cost, GA4, script-lift, RTB simulator, AI streaming, vendors, upload pages all render and integrate with backend. Found one CRITICAL backend bug: POST /api/upload/{dataset} performs no schema validation, so uploading an arbitrary CSV (e.g., vendor,score) into 'pmps' inserts malformed rows that subsequently break /api/pmps, /api/dashboard/overview, and /api/ai/recommendations (KeyError: 'outcome_adjusted_score' / 'verified_reach'). After hitting POST /api/admin/reseed, the system fully recovers.

critical.issue: No schema validation. Inserting any CSV (e.g. vendor,score) into 'pmps' dataset corrupts the collection. Subsequent GET /api/pmps, GET /api/dashboard/overview, and POST /api/ai/recommendations return HTTP 500 with KeyError: 'outcome_adjusted_score' / 'verified_reach' because endpoints assume seed-shape documents. Real users can permanently break the dashboard by uploading via the Data Upload page until an admin calls /api/admin/reseed.

minor.issue: Endpoint accepts an empty CSV without rows and returns rows_inserted:0 — should probably 400 on empty/malformed input.

Errors inside the streaming generator are swallowed and yielded as plain text (HTTP 200). Frontend can't distinguish a real LLM failure from a successful response. Consider yielding a typed error or setting an X-AI-Error header.
Defensively guard for empty/malformed pmps docs (use .get with default) so a single bad row doesn't 500 the entire dashboard.

integration_issues.issue: After a user uploads any CSV to the 'pmps' dataset via /upload, the Dashboard page shows an Uncaught runtime error overlay (Axios 500 from /api/dashboard/overview). Root cause is the backend upload bug above; UI also lacks any error boundary / friendly fallback for the dashboard.

AIRecommendations.jsx checks resp.ok but the backend always returns 200 (errors are streamed as text). When the LLM fails mid-stream the user sees a partial '[AI recommendation engine unavailable: ...]' message with no UI indication of failure.

action_items: Add Pydantic schema validation per dataset in POST /api/upload/{dataset} (only insert rows that match expected columns; reject the upload otherwise with 400 and clear field-level errors).
Make /api/dashboard/overview, /api/pmps, /api/vendors and /api/ai/recommendations resilient to malformed docs (use .get('outcome_adjusted_score', 0), filter out incomplete rows).
Consider returning a structured error event (or non-200 status) from /api/ai/recommendations when the LLM call fails so the UI can render a proper error state.
Add an ErrorBoundary around the dashboard route so a 500 doesn't break the whole app shell with a red runtime overlay.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py /api/upload accepts any column shape and pushes raw dicts to Mongo — no validation, no allowlist of columns; high blast radius since reads assume the seed schema.
All GET endpoints read with {'_id': 0} (good) and exclude ObjectId — OK.
ensure_seed() is idempotent via seed_meta doc, but only the seed_meta doc gates seeding. If a user uploads garbage into pmps the seed will NOT re-run automatically — operational footgun.
Frontend api.js has no global axios interceptor for errors; every page silently fails on rejected promises (Dashboard .then(setData) has no .catch).
AIRecommendations.jsx assumes resp.ok means a real LLM response but backend wraps LLM failures in 200 + plain text — UX gap.
Layout/Dashboard pages have data-testid on all KPIs and charts — good test coverage hooks.

success_rate.frontend: 100% routes render, all KPIs/charts/sliders/AI streaming verified after reseed

seed_data_creation: Backend auto-seeds on startup (idempotent via seed_meta). Called POST /api/admin/reseed once during testing to recover from the upload-induced data corruption.
context_for_next_testing_agent: If you hit 500s on /api/dashboard/overview or /api/pmps, the pmps collection has been corrupted by a CSV upload — POST /api/admin/reseed to recover. Backend test suite at /app/backend/tests/test_pharmasignal.py is intentionally simple: a vendor,score CSV upload into 'pmps' is the trigger for the bug we found. After main agent fixes the upload validation, re-run pytest and the frontend dashboard should remain stable even after running TestUpload.

rca of the issue.bug: Dashboard/PMPs/AI endpoints return 500 after CSV upload to pmps dataset.
rca of the issue.root_cause: /api/upload/{dataset} in server.py (lines 552-578) blindly insert_many's any parsed CSV rows into db[dataset] without validating columns. Inserting {'vendor': 'X', 'score': 88} into pmps means subsequent reads call p['outcome_adjusted_score']/p['verified_reach'] and raise KeyError, which becomes a 500.
rca of the issue.reproduction: 1) Start with seeded DB. 2) curl -F file=@small.csv https://.../api/upload/pmps where small.csv has columns vendor,score. 3) GET /api/dashboard/overview -> 500. 4) GET /api/pmps -> 500. 5) POST /api/ai/recommendations -> 500.
rca of the issue.mitigation: Either (a) define a Pydantic model per dataset and validate every row before insert, dropping or rejecting non-conforming rows, or (b) defensively use .get('field', 0) across all read endpoints and skip rows missing required keys. Long-term, do both.
